items.title: Alleged killer's recorded prison calls played to murder trial

children.content: Recorded prison phone calls — in which the former partner of a woman confronts her alleged murderer — have been played in South Australia's Supreme Court.

gallery.altText: A bearded man with a dog.

thumbnail.altText: A bearded man with a dog.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Legal action dropped against Ann Marie Smith's NDIS provider

children.content: Federal Court proceedings have been dropped against the former company responsible for caring for South Australian woman Ann Marie Smith, who died six years ago.

image.altText: A house converted into an office

items.title: Staff shortage led to woman being strangled by wheelchair belt, court hears

children.content: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

image.altText: The Coroners Court sign in Adelaide.

items.title: 'Falling apart': Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating

children.content: Parts of the Sturt Highway, which connects Sydney to Adelaide and provides an integral part of the country's freight network, have been given the lowest possible safety rating by the Australian Road Assessment Program (AusRAP).

image.altText: A man in a collared shirt stands arms crossed in front of a large freight truck.

items.title: Australian lamb new favourite in US as protein 'wave' creates demand

children.content: Australians may love their lamb, but it has always been a harder sell for American consumers. There are signs that is changing.

image.altText: Picture of lamb with the American flag

items.title: 'Gatekeeping septuagenarians': 'Inappropriate' TikTok video deleted

children.content: The National Trust of SA says one of its volunteers apologised after posting a social media clip labelling a group of disaffected members "gatekeeping septuagenarians" who were running a "stuffy museum".

image.altText: "Sex is great... but have you ever experienced the euphoria of ripping down every single no photo sign in a stuffy museum?"

items.title: Antisemitic attack on Adelaide home 'un-Australian', Jewish group says

children.content: The Jewish Community Council of South Australia condemns an act of vandalism committed on a man's Adelaide home as police continue to investigate.

image.altText: A police car parked in front of a house with fence graffitied with offensive language.

items.title: Bird flu detection training to be rolled out to volunteers

children.content: Training to help volunteers identify potential cases of bird flu will be rolled out in South Australia, under a partnership between conservation groups and government.

image.altText: A flock of crested terns in flight.

items.title: Greyhound industry reaches SA deadline after abuse revelations

children.content: A final report on greyhound industry compliance with improved standards following revelations of widespread abuse is expected to be handed to the SA government in coming months.

image.altText: Greyhound legs and lower torso while the animal is running.

items.title: Coroner calls for change after man died while paramedic waited outside

children.content: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

image.altText: A green and yellow ambulance driving on a road

items.title: Ex-cop turned missing persons expert says he may know where Peter Falconio is

children.content: Jim Whitehead has helped to close some of Australia's most high-profile missing persons cases, including those of Daniel Morcombe and Allison Baden-Clay. Now he is looking to help find the remains of British backpacker Peter Falconio.

articles.title: In Barrow Creek a $5 note hangs on the pub's wall, still waiting for Peter Falconio

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend, Joanne Lees, in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

image.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.

gallery.altText: A man sitting at a table holding a large paper map in his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A man sitting at a table holding a large paper map in his hands.

items.title: SA doctor banned for inappropriate relationship with client

children.content: A former Adelaide doctor will not be able to reapply for registration for at least two years after he was banned for having an inappropriate relationship with his client.

image.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

items.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say

children.content: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

image.altText: A drone of houses with mountain range behind them.

items.title: One dead after suspected carbon monoxide poisoning in Mount Gambier

children.content: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

image.altText: Emergency services vehicles in Mount Gambier.

items.title: Four more charges dropped against Alan Jones

children.content: With a months-long hearing due to begin in early August, Sydney's Downing Centre Local Court is conducting several weeks of pre-trial hearings.

image.altText: An old man in a suit looks downward while standing next to a car and surrounded by other men in suits.

items.title: Shark threat closes popular beaches, snorkelling spots along Ningaloo coast

children.content: Authorities save one animal but another succumbs to its injuries, leading sharks to converge on a popular snorkelling spot.

gallery.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

thumbnail.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

items.title: Sydney radio ratings reveal KIIS on the slide without Kyle and Jackie O

children.content: The KIIS Sydney audience has slipped further in the radio ratings in the latest blow to ARN after the departure of renowned breakfast radio juggernaut Kyle and Jackie O.

image.altText: Kyle Sandilands speaks into a gold-coloured microphone in a radio studio.

items.title: 'I'm lucky I'm here': Former building CEO details toll of being CFMEU 'target'

children.content: The former boss of Master Builders Queensland has told an inquiry he tried to take his own life after a campaign of intimidation by the Construction, Forestry and Maritime Employees Union.

image.altText: Two men stand in a tree with one holding a coffin.

items.title: How our property obsession killed productivity

children.content: With housing prices coming off the boil, maybe our banks will start lending to more productive areas of the economy, like business.

image.altText: A series of houses in Sydney with numerous dollar signs edited into the picture next to the houses.

items.title: Sam Neill on his career, dying and the role he turned down

children.content: Looking back at the wisdom and insight of the beloved New Zealand actor.

articles.title: 'Jurassic family': Spielberg leads outpouring of tributes for actor Sam Neill

children.content: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

image.altText: Sam Neill in character as Dr Alan Grant in Jurassic Park, standing in the rain wearing an akubra.

articles.title: Sam Neill leaves a wealth of films behind. Here are some of his best

children.content: With more than 150 acting roles under his belt, Sam Neill leaves a lasting legacy to the film and TV industry, and an array of characters that will stay in fans' hearts forever.

image.altText: Sam Neill and three actors in front of the Dish

items.title: Mixed martial arts group attempts to gift $82k cognac to Brisbane Olympic committee

children.content: The gifts, revealed exclusively by the ABC today, are part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

image.altText: a bottle of cognac next to two glasses on top of a barrell

items.title: Police charge alleged organiser behind Melbourne hospitality arson attacks

children.content: Investigators say the 20-year-old man was a high-ranking member of The Commission, an organised crime group believed to have targeted the city's nightlife venues in recent months.

image.altText: A police officer arresting a man

items.title: The impacts of caring for a family member as a young person

children.content: Kenny and Naomi were both younger than 10 when they became carers for family members.

items.title: Don't ask? Don't get: Five places to negotiate your bills to save money

children.content: Negotiating to reduce your spending and increase your income are tangible ways you can improve your financial situation right now.

image.altText: A concerned woman sits at table with a notebook. The background is illustrated with money symbols.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Your challenge in the ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

image.altText: A pair of dogs wear Norway's flag, a Viking helmet and a blond ponytail hairpiece suggestive of striker Erling Haaland's hair

items.title: How property developers are benefiting from Australia's housing crisis

children.content: State and federal governments are offering incentives for property developers in exchange for more affordable housing. But a Four Corners investigation has uncovered major flaws in the strategy.

items.title: 'Coordinator' of Sydney grandfather's mistaken kidnapping murder charged

children.content: A man accused of coordinating the alleged murder and kidnapping of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian has been charged.

image.altText: A man with long dark hair sits on the ground. A police officer in riot squad uniform stands over him.

items.title: ICE agents shoot and kill driver who witnesses say 'tried to stop'

children.content: A federal immigration officer has fatally shot a motorist in Maine, the second time in a week that US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents have used deadly force.

image.altText: A woman at a park holding a sign saying 'ICE not welcome here'

items.title: Signs of sugar detected near centre of the Milky Way

children.content: Astronomers have detected signs of a type of sugar found in raspberries in gas clouds near the centre of our galaxy the Milky Way.

items.title: Spain 'here to suffer' and France unites against racism ahead of World Cup semi

children.content: Spain is invoking the aura of the Roman Empire, while France is lighting up the pitch and remaining united off it, ahead of their men's World Cup semi-final in Dallas.

image.altText: A composite image showing France's Kylian Mbappe and Spain's Lamine Yamal celebrating

items.title: Former AFL player remembered as inspiration and 'great human' who loved life

children.content: A former AFL player has been remembered for his larger-than-life personality after being hit by a car and killed on Saturday night in Tasmania.

image.altText: A man poses in a football guernsey.

items.title: Man who sneaked into Australia 45 years ago has lived in limbo until now

children.content: Kwong Wan Lo stowed away on a ship bound for Australia 45 years ago. With no legal right to stay, he built a life in Sydney anyway.

A headshot of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a hat and sitting in an office.

items.title: Vehicle seized but no arrests after fatal Logan hit-and-run

children.content: A 20-year-old pedestrian died after he was struck on Middle Road at Greenbank shortly before 9pm.

image.altText: a suburban roundabout

items.title: Ravenhall and Fulham prison guards walk off job in fight for better pay

children.content: CPSU members at two Victorian prisons are striking today, asking for pay increases and parental leave.

image.altText: Image of a group of people standing outside, holding white and red union flags.

items.title: Deadly flashover the 'point of no return' for trapped pub patrons: Expert

children.content: A video analysis of an explosive pub fire in Thailand that killed 28 people appears to show a "flashover" that would have made evacuation almost impossible, an expert says.

image.altText: Flames erupt from pub entrance at nighttime as patrons flee onto street.
